messages.subject: [R-SIG-Finance] Issue with time_summarise function from tibbletime
messages.subject_clean: Issue with time_summarise function from tibbletime
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I'm trying to compute the 52week high and low and put each in a new
column.  I'm using the code below with BHP as an example.  However, I get
an error: time_summarise does not exist.  I'm using R v4.4.1 on OSX Big Sur
v11.7.1.

# Source - https://stackoverflow.com/a/47410716
# Posted by Hakki, modified by community. See post 'Timeline' for change
history
# Retrieved 2026-04-02, License - CC BY-SA 3.0

library(tidyquant)
library(tibbletime)

bhp <- tq_get("bhp")
bhp_tbltime <- tbl_time(bhp, index=date)

bhp_tbltime %>% mutate(date = collapse_index(date, "yearly")) %>%
        group_by(date) %>%
        time_summarise(high = max(adjusted),
                                    low = min(adjusted))

    roll_max <- rollify(max, window = 252)
    roll_min <- rollify(min, window = 252)

    bhp_tbltime %>%
      mutate(max252 = roll_max(adjusted),
             min252 = roll_min(adjusted)) %>%
      tail()

> bhp <- tq_get("bhp")
> bhp_tbltime <- tbl_time(bhp, index=date)
> bhp_tbltime %>% mutate(date = collapse_index(date, "yearly")) %>%
+         group_by(date) %>%
+         time_summarise(high = max(adjusted),
+                        low = min(adjusted))
... ... ... Error in time_summarise(., high = max(adjusted), low =
min(adjusted)) :
  could not find function "time_summarise"

TIA,

--
Andr? Luiz Tietbohl Ramos, PhD

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
